node.natural_language_proof:
Proof: let `S = Set.range u`. Since `u` is bounded above, `S` is bounded above, and it is nonempty,
so it has a least upper bound `l := sup S`. We claim `u` converges to `l`. Fix `eps > 0`. Then
`l - eps` is
not an upper bound of `S`, so there exists `N` with `u N > l - eps`. By monotonicity, for all
`n ≥ N` we have `u n ≥ u N > l - eps`, while always `u n ≤ l` since `l` is an upper bound. Thus for
`n ≥ N` we get `|u n - l| < eps`, so `Tendsto u atTop (nhds l)`.

theorem tendsto_of_monotone_bddAbove (u : ℕ → ℝ) (hu_mono : Monotone u) (hu_bdd : BddAbove (Set.range u)) :

node.natural_language_proof:
Proof: let `S = Set.range u`. Since `u` is bounded above, `S` is bounded above, and it is nonempty,
so it has a least upper bound `l := sup S`. We claim `u` converges to `l`. Fix `eps > 0`. Then
`l - eps` is
not an upper bound of `S`, so there exists `N` with `u N > l - eps`. By monotonicity, for all
`n ≥ N` we have `u n ≥ u N > l - eps`, while always `u n ≤ l` since `l` is an upper bound. Thus for
`n ≥ N` we get `|u n - l| < eps`, so `Tendsto u atTop (nhds l)`.
